summary: Recent changes to the 2D graphics area of Firefox focus on improving performance and reliability for various graphic tasks. One significant enhancement includes the creation of a **faster path in gfxFontGroup::ComputeRanges**, which should make text rendering quicker and more efficient. Additionally, changes to the handling of video features involved shifting from an allowlist to a blocklist for HDR (High Dynamic Range) video, which helps ensure only supported content is processed, thereby reducing errors. Optimizations in how the browser handles multi-GPU setups by enumerating outputs on all adapters will also contribute to more effective graphics rendering performance across different hardware configurations. _Overall, these updates enhance the user experience by streamlining graphics processes and preventing potential playback issues._
